transmitter.latitudeInvalid: Please enter a valid latitude (-90 to 90).
transmitter.longitudeInvalid: Please enter a valid longitude (-180 to 180).
transmitter.powerInvalid: Power must be a positive number.
transmitter.powerDbmTitle: Transmitter power in dBm. Converts to/from watts automatically (30 dBm = 1 W).
transmitter.frequencyInvalid: Frequency must be a positive number.
transmitter.heightTitle: Transmitter height above ground in meters (>= 1.0).
transmitter.heightInvalid: Height must be a positive number.
transmitter.gainInvalid: Gain must be a positive number.
transmitter.lossInvalid: Loss must be a positive number.
transmitter.setWithMapPopover: Click on the map to set the transmitter location.
transmitter.noNodeSelected: No node selected. Add a node to edit its parameters.
transmitter.invalidCoordsAlert: Please enter valid Latitude and Longitude values.

loRaPreset.modemPresetInfo: Shared by all nodes; sets the receiver sensitivity used to judge link viability. Pick a preset, or hand-edit the spreading factor / bandwidth below if your setup doesn't match one.
loRaPreset.applyFrequencyTitle: Set every node's frequency to this preset's frequency. Only available for MeshCore presets, which set a region frequency; Meshtastic presets and Custom don't define one.

simulation.maxRangeInvalid: Radius must be a positive number (default: 30 km).
simulation.qualityInfo: Sets the coverage overlay's target cell size and the link-profile sampling detail. The target cell size only holds out to the range its texture budget allows; past that, cells grow with range (see the readout below). Applies to the link matrix, profile, coverage and relay.
simulation.overlayResolutionInfo: Caps how large the draped coverage image can get, so it bounds the smallest cell at long range. Standard hits 8 m cells out to ~16 km; High reaches 8 m across the full range but uses much more memory and may not render on older Safari. Clamped to your GPU's texture limit.
simulation.coverageCellsPrefix: Coverage cells at the current range:
simulation.filterHorizonInfo: When computing the link matrix, skip pairs beyond the radio horizon — the line-of-sight distance set by the curve of the Earth and each node's height above sea level (higher nodes reach further). Turn off for non-line-of-sight bands.
simulation.maxLinkDistanceInfo: Hard cap for “Compute all” only: skip node pairs farther apart than this, even if the horizon allows them. Useful on dense maps where hilltop nodes have very long horizons. 0 = no limit. Computing a single node's links (L) always checks every node.

listener.heightAglInfo: Height above ground assumed for a listener elsewhere on the map when generating coverage — not any particular node's own antenna height (see the node's Transmitter panel for that).
listener.heightAglInvalid: Height must be a positive number.

linkMatrix.computeSingleTitle: Compute links from the selected node to every other node (fast). Shortcut: L
linkMatrix.cancelTitle: Stop the running link computation (keeps any links already computed)
linkMatrix.computeAllTitle: Compute every node-to-node link (slower on large maps)
linkMatrix.onlySelectedLinksInfo: Hide every link except those touching the selected node. Off: viable links always show, marginal/failed links only for the selected node.
linkMatrix.hideInvalidLinksInfo: Hide links that don't meet the margin threshold, including those touching the selected node.
linkMatrix.needTwoNodes: Add at least two nodes to compute links.
linkMatrix.selectNodePrompt: Select a node to see its links.
linkMatrix.uncalculatedOne: 1 node not yet calculated - compute this node's links
linkMatrix.uncalculatedMany: {count} nodes not yet calculated - compute this node's links
linkMatrix.confirmFullMatrix:
Computing every link between {n} nodes can take a while and download a lot of terrain. For a single node, use “Calculate node's links” (or press L) instead.

Compute the full matrix anyway?

relayFinder.needTwoNodes: Add at least two nodes to find a relay site.
relayFinder.promoteHint: Each suggested point can be promoted into a real node.
relayFinder.pickTwoAndSearch: Pick two nodes and search for a relay site.
relayFinder.noOverlap: No location receives both A and B above sensitivity.

viewshed.description: A fast, in-browser line-of-sight checker: everything the selected node can see is tinted green. It reads whatever surface the map's 3D terrain is currently showing (bare-earth DEM, surface DSM, or the simulation grid), so switch terrain source in Settings to compare. This is a quick approximation - the Coverage (SPLAT) run remains the authoritative radio model.
viewshed.unsupported: Viewshed needs a browser with WebGPU or WebGL2 support. The rest of the app is unaffected.
viewshed.addOrSelectNode: Add or select a node to begin.
viewshed.computeError: Couldn't compute the viewshed — see the console. Try a smaller radius.
viewshed.liveRecomputeInfo: On = recompute continuously as you drag the node (needs a fast GPU; runs at lower detail mid-drag). Off = recompute when you drop it or change a setting.
viewshed.radiusInfo: How far out to test. Larger radius = coarser terrain at this range.
viewshed.receiverHeightInfo: Height above ground at the tested cells. The observer uses the node's antenna height.

profilePanel.shareTitle: Copy a share link for this link profile

measurePanel.title: Measure
measurePanel.clickToStart: Click the map to start measuring.
measurePanel.clickToAddPoints: Click to add points; double-click to finish.

contactImport.title: Import contacts
contactImport.info: Pick a MeshCore contacts export (JSON). Repeaters and room servers that carry a location are added as nodes in an Imported folder; companions and contacts without a location are skipped. You can review and deselect before importing.
contactImport.duplicateTitle: A node with this name and location already exists
contactImport.noneWithLocation: No repeaters or room servers with a location ({count} had none).
contactImport.noneFound: No repeaters or room servers found in this file.
contactImport.notJson: Could not read this file as JSON.
contactImport.readError: Could not read the selected file.

publicMapSync.title: Sync public map
publicMapSync.info: Pulls repeaters & room servers in the current map view from the public mesh maps into a Public MeshCore folder. Pan and zoom to the area you want first, then sync. Nodes already on the map are skipped, so you can re-sync freely.
publicMapSync.mapNotReady: The map is not ready yet.
publicMapSync.allAlreadyPresent: All {count} nodes in view are already on the map.
publicMapSync.noneFound: No repeaters or room servers found in the current view.
publicMapSync.confirmAdd: This will add {count} nodes to "Public MeshCore". Zoom in to narrow the area, or continue?
publicMapSync.meshMapperCapped: Large area — fetched only the {count} nearest MeshMapper regions. Zoom in for full coverage.
publicMapSync.meshMapperFailedOne: 1 MeshMapper region could not be fetched.
publicMapSync.meshMapperFailedMany: {count} MeshMapper regions could not be fetched.

providerForm.placeholdersHelp: Must include {z}/{x}/{y}; include any API key directly in the URL.

demProviders.title: Terrain providers
demProviders.info: Higher-detail elevation layered over the Mapterhorn baseline wherever a provider has data — the baseline still fills the rest of the world, so enabling one only adds detail, never removes coverage. Feeds the line-of-sight / coverage sims, viewshed, 3D terrain mesh, and hillshade. Where two providers overlap, the one lower in this list wins. LINZ rows are pre-configured and NZ-only (elevation © LINZ, CC‑BY 4.0). OSM Buildings adds OpenStreetMap building footprint heights on top of the ground everywhere in the world, so buildings actually block links/coverage/viewshed, independent of the visual 3D-buildings map layer. While that visual layer is also on, its bump is left out of the 3D terrain mesh/hillshade specifically (its raster resolution makes a messy blocky mound sitting behind the sharp building shapes) — turn the visual layer off and the bump reappears there as the only visual sign of buildings. Add your own region's DEM/DSM tile service below — it needs to serve Mapbox or Terrarium-encoded terrain-RGB PNG tiles, the same scheme LINZ uses.

terrain.toggle3dInfo: Toggle 3D terrain with the 3D button on the map (top-left, under the basemap buttons). It drapes the map over an elevation mesh (Mapterhorn); tilt with right-drag (or the compass control) to read hill elevation. The Shade button beside it adds relief shading that reads hills on flat basemaps — works in both top-down and 3D view.
terrain.nzBasemapInfo: Adds a Satellite (NZ) option to the basemap buttons, using LINZ aerial photography — higher detail than the global Esri imagery, but New Zealand only (blank elsewhere). Off by default; enable it if you're mapping in NZ. Imagery © LINZ, CC‑BY 4.0.
terrain.shadingIntensityInfo: Multidirectional relief shading from the DEM. Higher = stronger.
terrain.verticalExaggerationInfo: Higher values make subtle terrain easier to read.
terrain.show3dLinksInfo: Draw links as 3D lines flying through the air between antenna tops, with terrain-clipping sections in yellow. When off, links stay as flat lines draped on the ground.
terrain.showDropCurtainInfo: A translucent wall dropped from each link to the ground, showing its track and where it clips terrain.
terrain.dragOnlySelectedInfo: When on, dragging a marker only moves the currently selected node — other pins near your cursor won't move by accident. Turn off to make every unlocked node draggable, as before. Works alongside the node lock button on the map, which disables dragging entirely.
terrain.flatSignalLineInfo: In the Link Profile chart, draw the signal line straight and let the terrain curve up/down instead — the same physical geometry, just flipped so the terrain shows Earth's curvature rather than the signal.

mapControls.toggle3dTerrainTitle: Toggle 3D terrain (tilt the map to see relief)
mapControls.toggleShadingTitle: Toggle terrain shading (relief / ambient-occlusion look). Works in flat and 3D view.
mapControls.toggleBuildingsTitle: Toggle 3D buildings (OpenStreetMap) — shows from zoom 14 in
mapControls.toggleLinksTitle: Toggle 3D line-of-sight links (lines flying through the air between antenna tops, clipping sections in yellow)
mapControls.viewshedOnTitle: Toggle the line-of-sight viewshed — green where the selected node has clear LOS
mapControls.viewshedOffTitle: Select a node to show its viewshed
mapControls.nodesLockedTitleDesktop: Nodes locked in place — click to allow dragging
mapControls.nodesLockedTitleTouch: Nodes locked in place — tap to allow dragging
mapControls.nodesLockedAriaLabelTouch: Nodes locked — tap to allow dragging
mapControls.lockNodesTitle: Lock nodes in place to prevent accidental dragging
